mnemonics: I-T-S-C-T: Insertion, Traversal, Searching, Copying Trees - Imagine Trees Should Contain Carefully Transplanted items
keyPoints: Insertion follows BST rules (left subtree < root < right subtree)
In-order traversal produces sorted output for BSTs
Search efficiency depends on tree balance (O(log n) best, O(n) worst)
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update parent pointers during insertion
Using wrong traversal type for desired output order
Creating shallow copies instead of deep copies when duplicating trees
